issue_title: Access Token is not active when retrieved via token exchange
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Token Exchange does not seem to work correctly when you are exchanging for a new subject.

An access token is returned, but then when you introspect it, it is not active. This causes libraries like nest-keycloak-connect to be unusable with the new tokens as you get correctly get unauthorized exceptions.

I have double-checked that the host is the same as the issuer and the introspecter, and I have been running tests via Postman with no luck.

Below is a screenshot of how I am exchanging via postman:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3884418/147261518-420a9c84-6a36-496e-931e-dc8e9b37ac4c.png)


### Version

15.0.1

### Expected behavior

The Access Token and Id Token should be able to access applications just as if the user had manually logged in.

### Actual behavior

When the access token is introspected, it falsely reports that the token is not active.

`{"active":false}`

### How to Reproduce?

Generate an access token for your admin user.
Use that token to exchange the token for another user token.
Try and use the new token with token/introspect

See that it doesn't work.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow introspecting tokens issued during token exchange with delegation semantics

Closes #9337

issue_title: Definition of keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist in keycloak-adapter-bom doesn't work
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist is a zip-file artifact. The definition in keycloak-adapter-bom doesn't set the type, so it defaults to jar.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

keycloak-adapter-bom probably shouldn't include keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist. It's a zip-file artifact, I don't see why one would include it as a dependency. But maybe I just don't know the use case?

Alternatively, keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist could be included with <type>zip</zip> to make it work.

### Actual behavior

Error:
```
[ERROR]   The project com.example:keycloak-bom-dist:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT (...) has 1 error
[ERROR]     'dependencies.dependency.version' for org.keycloak:keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist:zip is missing. @ line 29, colum
n 21
[ERROR]
```

### How to Reproduce?

Run `mvn package` on the following POM:
```
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd"
>
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>keycloak-bom-dist</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>

    <properties>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <maven.compiler.source>8</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>${maven.compiler.source}</maven.compiler.target>
    </properties>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.keycloak.bom</groupId>
                <artifactId>keycloak-adapter-bom</artifactId>
                <version>16.1.0</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.keycloak</groupId>
            <artifactId>keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist</artifactId>
<!--             <version>16.1.0</version> -->
            <type>zip</type>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</project>
```


### Anything else?

keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist in keycloak-adapter-bom:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/16.1.0/boms/adapter/pom.xml#L52
commit_message:
Remove keycloak-wildfly-adapter-dist from adapter bom (#9346)

The artifact is a zip-file. The definition in the bom doesn't set the
type, so it defaults to jar which doesn't work.

Fixes #9339

issue_title: Expected Scopes of ClientScopesCondition created on Admin UI are not saved onto ClientScopesCondition.Configuration
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

On Admin Console - Realm Settings - Client Policies - Policies - Form View - [some policy], we created client scope condition and set some scope value to Expected Scopes.
After that, I sent an authorization request to keycloak with scope including one in Expected Scopes.
I expect that this request would be processed by the policy. However, keycloak's client scope condition judges "No".

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

I've already found out root cause of this issue and how to resolve it.

ClientScopesConditionFactory :
```
    public static final String SCOPES = "scopes";
```

ClientScopesCondition:
```
    public static class Configuration extends ClientPolicyConditionConfigurationRepresentation {

        protected String type;
        protected List<String> scope;
```

ClientScopesConditionFactory 's SCOPES value must much with ClientScopesCondition.Configuration 's scope field name.

In this case, 
```
        protected List<String> scope;
```
should be refactored as (also its getter/setter)
```
        protected List<String> scopes

        public List<String> getScopes() {
            return scopes;
        }

        public void setScopes(List<String> scopes) {
            this.scopes = scopes;
        }
```

Keycloak's UI codes read ClientScopesConditionFactory's property and its name, construct JSON representation automatically using this name. In this case,
```
          "configuration": {
            "scopes": [
              "read_account",
              "dummy"
            ],
            "type": "Optional"
```

However, the current keycloak tries to parse "scope" not "scopes" because current ClientScopesCondition.Configuration's field name is "scope".

Workaround is as follows.

On Admin UI, open JSON editor and rename "scopes" to "scope" like
```
          "configuration": {
            "scope": [
              "read_account",
              "dummy"
            ],
            "type": "Optional"
```

However, "scopes" might be appropriate as its representation, not "scope".
commit_message:
Expected Scopes of ClientScopesCondition created on Admin UI are not saved onto ClientScopesCondition.Configuration

Closes #9371

issue_title: Client secret length should be adjusted based on select signature algorithm
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Client secret length should be changed based on Mac Algorithm if secret-jwt is used as Client Authenticator.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7783488/147913805-4b9e5f45-80f2-449f-994e-3cedb3818a54.png)

Currently, Keycloak generates client secret with 32 characters which is only good for HS256 (32 x 8 = 256). 

However if user wants to use HS384 or HS512, secret length should be changed based on selected signature algorithm as client secret is used to sign the JWT.

For HS384, secret length should be 48 (384 / 8 = 48). And For HS512, secret length should be 64 (512 / 8 = 64).


### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

Secret length should be changed based on selected signature algorithm.

### Actual behavior

Secret length is always 32 characters which can only work with HS256 signature algorithm.

### How to Reproduce?

- Select `Signed Jwt with Client Secret` as client authenticator for OIDC client
- Select HS512 as signature algorithm
- Regenerate the secret and we can see it is only 32 characters long

```java
//Reference: org.keycloak.models.utils.KeycloakModelUtils#generateSecret
public static String generateSecret(ClientModel client) {
        String secret = SecretGenerator.getInstance().randomString();
        client.setSecret(secret);
        return secret;
}
```
commit_message:
Add flow to generate secret length based on signature algorithm (#13107)

Closes #9376

You can connect to the database

### Actual behavior

Try to connect to an external postgres database with a command like:

issue_title: JPA-Map storage might loose writes due to missing locking mechanism
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The JPA implementation for the map storage provider doesn't specify a locking strategy, therefore writes might get lost: When Hibernate writes the contents back to the database, the contents might have been updated by another concurrent request already.
In an extreme case this could lead to some column being updated while others aren't, leaving the database in an inconsistent state. 



### Version

17.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When persisting data in the JPA map storage provider 
succeeds, the contents should be stored consistently in all related tables (main entity table plus additional tables for searching). 

When a concurrent transaction modifies the data, the transaction should fail with an exception. 

### Actual behavior

When a concurrent write occurred, Hibernate will overwrite them without noticing some other transaction has modified the data in between. As Hibernate will write only content to the database it considered changed, this could lead to a partial overwrite of the concurrent transaction, leading to inconsistent data.

### How to Reproduce?

Looking at the code, no JPA entity of the map storage has a version column. The option of `optimistic-lock="all"` is not used AFAIK.

While the old JPA storage uses PESSIMISTIC_WRITE in several places (usually when deleting data), there is no such code in the new JPA storage provider.



### Anything else?

**Inside of the scope of this issue:** The old storage uses PESSIMISTIC_WRITE in several places, find out the rationale why it did so, and if this should apply to the new JPA map storage as well.

**Outside of the scope of this issue:** As the admin API of Keycloak doesn't provide a mechanism for optimistic locking or identification of concurrent updates by multiple clients (there is not version ID or similar in the API), lost writes when accessing the REST API might still occur. Adding a mechanism for that in the API of map storage and other APIs is out of scope here. This is only about keeping the JPA storage consistent.
commit_message:
JPA-Map storage might loose writes due to missing locking mechanism

Closes #9411

issue_title: Support kerberos realm filter on LDAP provider
issue_body:
TL;DR: Support a configuration on LDAP provider which would specify which Kerberos realm(s) are handled by given LDAP provider.

_Original description follows_

### Describe the bug

### [KEYCLOAK-7009](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-7009)
```
Keycloak supported multiple kerberos realms with [KEYCLOAK-3842](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-3842), but it does not work with the following situations.
```

### Closing reason
```
Closing as duplicate of [KEYCLOAK-6225](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-6225) . Although the "Steps to reproduce" are really nice and will help when we start work on [KEYCLOAK-6225](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-6225) (which is not clear when it will be).
```

### [KEYCLOAK-6225](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-6225)
```
In a scenario where multiple User Storage providers are configured, and you have 2 LDAP providers configured against Kerberos realm (assuming both use same keytab file). Keycloak ideally should look up after the user being authenticated in each one (following priority order) until find it.

For example, in a scenario where we have two providers: LDAP A and LDAP B, if the user couldn't be found in the first one, Keycloak would look up in LDAP B.

Today, if the user couldn't be found in the first one, the authentication flow stops and return an authentication error to the client.
```

### Closing reason
```
Issue is being closed as out of date due to lack of work on this issue.
```

I made a PR a while ago after asking on the mailing list, I can update it if you want.

### Version

15.1.1

### Expected behavior

User should be logged in on the correct ldap

Improvement plan:
-----------------

I think Keycloak should search the ldap which is extracted from the user principal.\
(I think active directory has a lot of configuration such as global catalog, so the inprovement plan should be discussed much here.)

### Actual behavior

(1)\
I found user002 and user003 can not do the desktop single sign on.\
Keycloak judges error when "Kerberos authenticatin is succeded" and "Keycloak cannot find the user information from the ldap provider".

server.log

2018-03-16 17:39:47,233 WARN  [org.keycloak.storage.ldap.LDAPStorageProvider] (default task-13) Kerberos/SPNEGO authentication succeeded with username [user002], but couldn't find or create user with federation provider [aaa.com]

(2)\
When the ccc.com\user001 logged in to the windows domain, desktop sso succeed but the Keycloak get the wrong user information ( aaa.com\user001 ).\
Keycloak got the user principal ( user001@ccc.com ) but Keycloak extract just "user001" and search the user information without using "ccc.com".

### How to Reproduce?

Situation:
----------

-   I have the multiple kerberos realms active directory environment attached.
-   I want to implemet a desktop single sign on using "ldap provider".

Active Directory setting:
-------------------------

-   Domain Controller : AAA.COM

    ktpass -out keycloak.HTTP.keytab.aaa -princ HTTP/keycloak.example.com@AAA.COM -ptype KRB5_NT_PRINCIPAL -mapuser keycloak -pass P@ssw0rd

-   Domain Controller : CCC.COM

    ktpass -out keycloak.HTTP.keytab.ccc -princ HTTP/keycloak.example.com@CCC.COM -ptype KRB5_NT_PRINCIPAL -mapuser keycloak -pass P@ssw0rd

Keycloak setting:
-----------------

### Keycloak User Federation:

#### 1\. aaa.com (priority:0)

| key | value |
|---|---|
| Username LDAP attribute | sAMAccountName |
| Connection URL | ldap://aaa.com |
| Allow Kerberos authentication | ON |
| Kerberos Realm | AAA.COM |
| Server Principal | HTTP/keycloak.example.com@AAA.COM |
| KeyTab | /opt/keytab/keycloak.HTTP.keytab.aaa |

#### 2\. bbb.com (priority:10)

| key | value |
|---|---|
| Username LDAP attribute | sAMAccountName |
| Connection URL | ldap://bbb.com |
| Allow Kerberos authentication | ON |
| Kerberos Realm | BBB.COM |
| Server Principal | HTTP/keycloak.example.com@AAA.COM |
| KeyTab | /opt/keytab/keycloak.HTTP.keytab.aaa |

#### 3\. ccc.com (priority:20)

| key | value |
|---|---|
| Username LDAP attribute | sAMAccountName |
| Connection URL | ldap://ccc.com |
| Allow Kerberos authentication | ON |
| Kerberos Realm | CCC.COM |
| Server Principal | HTTP/keycloak.example.com@CCC.COM |
| KeyTab | /opt/keytab/keycloak.HTTP.keytab.ccc |

![_thumb_12433272](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4395018/148535554-31d2f359-a66a-4358-a482-ba82ac85beb2.png)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Login with Github errors with class cast exception when user chooses to keep their email addresses private
issue_body: ### Describe the bug Keycloak errors out with an IDENTITY_PROVIDER_LOGIN_ERROR and shows a 502 error page when a user chooses to login with Github. This only occurs when the user has decided to make their email address private on Github pages. Irrespective of what the user chooses to show or not show on their public Github account, an OIDC exchange for credentials should return back a email address if the scope has the appropriate entries (user:email, in this case). ### Version 15.0.2 ### Expected behavior Irrespective of whether the user opts to show their email on their public profile on Github, when doing an OIDC flow, the service provider should be able to access the user's email address if they include the appropriate scope parameter. ### Actual behavior ### How to Reproduce? 1. Connect an application that uses keycloak to provide the ability to 'Login with Github'. 2. Login with a Github account that has the box "Keep my email addresses private" checked. 3. Keycloak will return as error page. ### Anything else? Need to investigate how the user profile is accessed by keycloak during this exchange. Context: On GitHub profile settings, under Public email, some users (including myself) have that option set to "Don't show my email address." The call to /user only returns the public email address, so if the user doesn't want it shown, the API will respect that. To get the user's email addresses regardless of whether or not they are public, use the call to /user/emails with the appropriate access token included. issue_title: Client Policies : Condition's negative logic configuration is not shown in Admin Console's form view
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

On Admin Console - Realm Settings - Client Policies - Policies - Form View - [create policy] - [create condition], we tries to create any condition but negative logic setting is not shown.

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

Every condition have negative logic setting. Therefore, we expect the negative logic setting is shown if we tries to any condition on Form view.

### Actual behavior

The negative logic setting is not shown when we tries to create any condition.

### How to Reproduce?

On Admin Console - Realm Settings - Client Policies - Policies - Form View - [create policy] - [create condition], create any condition.

### Anything else?

I've already found out root cause of this issue.

Every condition's factory class does not add negative logic setting to properties in its static initializer. UI creates automatically its form by reading this properties that does not include negative logic setting.


Workaround is as follows.

On Admin UI, open JSON editor and add "is-negative-logic" boolean field to condition's configuration.
commit_message:
Client Policies : Condition's negative logic configuration is not shown in Admin Console's form view

Closes #9447

issue_title: Validation for CIBA binding_message parameter
issue_body:
### Description

The CIBA specification mentions this about `binding_message` parameter in this section https://openid.net/specs/openid-client-initiated-backchannel-authentication-core-1_0.html#rfc.section.7.1 :
```
Because the various devices involved may have limited display abilities and the message is intending for visual inspection by 
the end-user, the binding_message value SHOULD be relatively short and use a limited set of plain text characters. The 
invalid_binding_message defined in Section 13 is used in the case that it is necessary to inform the Client that the provided 
binding_message is unacceptable. 
```

Keycloak currently allows any binding message, but it may be better to limit this according to the specification. For example some other FAPI CIBA certified providers (Authlete, Raidiam) has limitation on the binding message to be limited in length and have only plain-text characters like this regex: `^[a-zA-Z0-9-._+/!?#]{1,50]$ `

Moreover, changing it this way will help to have FAPI CIBA automated conformance testsuite working (without restrictions on the binding message, the automated test `fapi-ciba-id1-ensure-authorization-request-with-potentially-bad-binding-message` is currently failing in the automated testsuite and testsuite always need to be run manually to pass)

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

_No response_

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: Placeholders in keycloak.properties do not get substituted at runtime after a build

### Describe the bug

0) use a `keycloak.properties` file like this:
```
%prod.db.username=${MY_DB_USERNAME}
%prod.db.password=${MY_DB_PASSWORD}

2) change the corresponding MY_DB_PASSWORD environment variable (and the password if you want)

New value from the newly set environment variable is used

### Actual behavior

Old value from last build is used

see description

this is due to the fact that persisted.properties has a higher ordinal than properties files, so the placeholder substitution gets again overwritten before applying the configuration

issue_title: Keycloak Server throws NPE at startup when the MAP_STORAGE feature is enabled
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the `MAP_STORAGE` feature is enabled (it doesn't have to be configured, just enabled), the server fails to start with a `NullPointerException`:

```
18:10:29,438 FATAL [org.keycloak.services] (ServerService Thread Pool -- 52) Error during startup: java.lang.NullPointerException
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-services@17.0.0-SNAPSHOT//org.keycloak.services.resources.KeycloakApplication$1.run(KeycloakApplication.java:134)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-private@17.0.0-SNAPSHOT//org.keycloak.models.utils.KeycloakModelUtils.runJobInTransaction(KeycloakModelUtils.java:239)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-services@17.0.0-SNAPSHOT//org.keycloak.services.resources.KeycloakApplication.startup(KeycloakApplication.java:128)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-wildfly-extensions@17.0.0-SNAPSHOT//org.keycloak.provider.wildfly.WildflyPlatform.onStartup(WildflyPlatform.java:36)
	at org.keycloak.keycloak-services@17.0.0-SNAPSHOT//org.keycloak.services.resources.KeycloakApplication.<init>(KeycloakApplication.java:114)

``` 
The point where the exception is thrown is this:

```java
                DBLockProvider dbLock = dbLockManager.getDBLock();
                dbLock.waitForLock(DBLockProvider.Namespace.KEYCLOAK_BOOT);
```
`dbLockManager.getDBLock()` is essentially doing `session.getProvider(DBLockProvider.class)`, which implies that Keycloak is unable to obtain the lock provider when the `MAP_STORAGE` feature is enabled.

This happens because the `NoLockingDBLockProviderFactory` that is enabled with the `MAP_STORAGE` feature ends up competing with the `LiqubaseDBLockProviderFactory` that already exists and Keycloak can't decide which factory to use. Explicitly configuring the default provider or setting the order of the `LiquibaseDBLockProviderFactory` to 1 fixes the issue. The latter is the preferred solution as it was applied to all legacy JPA storage providers so that they wouldn't clash with the MAP providers enabled by the `MAP_STORAGE` feature.

Tested using the `WildFly` distribution (under `distribution/server-dist`) and `Quarkus` (under `distribution/server-x-dist`). Using the `KeycloakServer` class, which runs on `Undertow`, works fine because the `keycloak-server.json` configuration used by this server contains explicit declarations of the default providers, including the `DBLockProvider`.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak server should be able to start without any errors when the `MAP_STORAGE` feature is enabled.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak server startup fails with a `NPE` when the feature is enabled.

### How to Reproduce?

Easiest way is to go to `distribution/server-dist/target/keycloak-x.y.z/bin` and start the server with the `-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.map_storage=enabled` flag:

```
./standalone.sh -Dkeycloak.profile.feature.map_storage=enabled

```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Set order of LiquibaseDBLockProviderFactory to 1

- makes it the default provider when no provider is explicitly configured
- avoid NPE at server startup when other providers are present and none is set as default

issue_title: Can't add user to "parent group" if member of a subgroup
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Trying to add a user to a parent group when the user already is a member of a subgroup don't work. No errors, just not added to the group.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

User gets added to the group or a error is thrown.

### Actual behavior

User is not added to the group and no visible errors.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new group.
2. Create a group under the newly created group.
3. Add a user to the subgroup created in step 2.
4. Try to add the user to the parent group created in step 1.

You will get `Success! Added group membership`, but the user is not added to the group. The same is true from the API.

The reverse order (add to the root group first) works as excepted.

### Anything else?

If this is working as expected the API should return a error, and doing it in reverse should not work.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/891048/148834534-d3cd163f-450d-44c5-b402-6a124e7015d4.png)
commit_message: Fixes #9482: A user could be assigned to a parent group if he is already assigned to a subgroup.

issue_title: Username editable when user is forced to re-authenticate
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

See https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-18905

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

- During user re-authentication, the username field should not be editable. And it may not even be visible due the fact that username is shown at the top of the login screen

- There should be notification that user is re-authenticating (not authenticating from the scratch)

- In case that realm has registration enabled, there should not be "Registration" link available during re-authentication (Currently it is)

- Same applies for remember-me link

- During re-authentication, only the IDPs, which are already linked to the user account, should be shown on the login screen. Currently all realm IDPs are shown, which is not correct

- In case of identity-first (Separate username authenticator shown first), the Username authenticator can be entirely skipped and the password authenticator may be shown as first screen. This is possible just in case that user is not linked to any IDPs (There is limitation that IDPs are currently shown on the username form authenticator, but not on the passwordForm authenticator, which is hopefully something we can improve with authentication policies)

issue_title: Newline in localization messages causes uncaught syntax error in account console v2
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

A properties file with the property `test=foo\nbar` leads to the following JavaScript code that is embedded in the HTML code of the account console v2 (`http://<keycloak>/auth/realms/<realm>/account/`):

```javascript
var l18nMsg = JSON.parse('{..., "test":"foo\nbar", ...}');
```

The string `foo\nbar` cannot be parsed by JavaScript and therefore a SyntaxError is raised which is shown in the developer console.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

Backslashes in the properties file must be escaped, e.g., `foo\nbar` must be serialized as `foo\\nbar`.

### Actual behavior

Backslashes in the properties file are not escaped which leads to a SyntaxError exception:

```javascript
> JSON.parse('{"foo": "\n"}')
VM536:1 Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token 
 in JSON at position 9
    at JSON.parse (<anonymous>)
    at <anonymous>:1:6
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a properties file `messages_en.properties` with the content `text=foo\nbar` in the directory `resources` and deploy the JAR file.
2. Open the account console v2.
3. Observe the exception in the developer console.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Rework escaping of special characters in message properties for account console (#9995)

Closes #9503

issue_title: Multiple warnings caused by typed varargs in TokenVerifier
issue_body:
### Description

The [org.keycloak.TokenVerifier::withChecks()](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/TokenVerifier.java#L295) method declaration causes the following warnings:
* a possible heap pollution warning at declaration site;
* 16 more unchecked generic array creation warnings at call sites in `keycloak-services`.

As we neither cast `checks` to `Object[]` nor rely on it being an instance of `Predicate[]`, it is safe to suppress the warning. issue_title: Scope bug in device authorization request
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

According to the [Device AuthZ Grant specification (see Section 3.1)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8628#section-3.1), the device authorization request supports requesting the scope of the access request through the `scope` (optional) parameter.

However, Keycloak does not honour well the `scope` parameter (optional client scopes). This affects:

- the consent which (when enabled) does not show the scopes requested in the device authorisation request

- the UserInfo endpoint after a successful Device Access Token Request does not contains the claims mapped to the requested scopes

- when the consent is enabled, the Device Access Token Request fails with the following message: { "error": "invalid_scope", "error_description": "Client no longer has requested consent from user"}

In order to solve this bug, requested scopes are added to 'scope' client note in authenticationSession after successfully verification.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Execute Device Authorization Request with scope parameter containing optional scopes of client.

For optional scope with consent 'Display On Consent Screen' equal to true bullet one and three will happen.

For optional scope with consent 'Display On Consent Screen' equal to false bullet two will happen.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Handling lazy loading exceptions for storage in new and old storage
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When lazy-loading information from the JPA Map storage, the test ConcurrencyTest#createRemoveClient() showed NPEs when a clients was removed while populating the list of clients. 

With the old JPA storage this was handled that all exceptions during lazy loading where caught and the entities involved would be removed from the list of clients returned to the caller using `try { c.getClientId(); return true; } catch (Exception ex) { return false; }`.

### Version

17.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

* Throw a specific exception related to lazy loading across all storage implementations
* Don't depend on a storage provider specific specific field as with`c.getClientId()` to trigger the exception
* Document the behavior of removing clients from the client list when a lazy loading exception occurs
* Handle lazy loading situations in map JPA storage implementation for deletion and modification of entities

### Actual behavior

* All exceptions being caught
* Provider specific field to trigger lazy loading problems
* No documentation present (neither for the caller, nor in the code)
* No handling of lazy loading issues in JPA map storage provider

### How to Reproduce?

Run ConcurrencyTest#createRemoveClient() with map-jpa storage configuration.

### Anything else?

There's been a GitHub discussion to find out what needed to be done: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9601
This will be applied in all places where the pattern of filtering out clients is used.

Also: 
* Implement similar handling for Map JPA Roles.
* Revisit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/model/map-jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/jpa/JpaMapStorageProviderFactory.java#L157-L166 to see if the isolation warning can now be dropped.

Out of scope: 
* Client will not be able to identify what clients have been removed - this is the same behavior we have today
* All illegal state exceptions will be handled the same way, both removed and modified clients will lead to the clients not being listed in the caller's result.
* No retrying of the request or return a 500 error, as this could potentially lead to a denial of service situation when a lot of clients are modified concurrently.

Added ModelIllegalStateException to handle lazy loading exception.

Closes #9645

Fix embedded theme-resources lookup in Keycloak.X

issue_title: Register event not triggered on external to internal token exchange
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If a user is created when using [external token to internal token exchange](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/#external-token-to-internal-token-exchange) no `EventType.REGISTER` is triggered

### Version

15.0.1 and 12.0.4

### Expected behavior

A register event should also be logged

### Actual behavior

No register event is triggered

### How to Reproduce?

Setup token exchange and login using a user that does not have an account in keycloak. Check the events.

We tested this with both Google and Facebook idp

### Anything else?

A register event is triggered if a keycloak user is created using the normal OAuth 2.0 flow so I expected it to be triggered on token exchange too

### Temporary fix:

Note: This will trigger 2 register events on the normal OAuth 2.0 flow and one register event on token exchange.

Add a new `IdentityProviderMapper` and trigger the event inside `importNewUser`:
```
public class TriggerRegisterUserEvent extends AbstractIdentityProviderMapper {
  public static final String[] COMPATIBLE_PROVIDERS = {"google", "facebook"};

  @Override
  public void importNewUser(
      KeycloakSession session,
      RealmModel realm,
      UserModel user,
      IdentityProviderMapperModel mapperModel,
      BrokeredIdentityContext context) {
    EventBuilder event =
        new EventBuilder(realm, session, session.getContext().getConnection())
            .event(EventType.REGISTER)
            .user(user)
            .client(session.getContext().getClient())
            .detail(Details.IDENTITY_PROVIDER, context.getIdpConfig().getAlias())
            .detail(Details.REGISTER_METHOD, "token-exchange")
            .detail(Details.IDENTITY_PROVIDER_USERNAME, context.getUsername());
    if (user.getEmail() != null) event.detail(Details.EMAIL, user.getEmail());
    event.success();
  }
  //...
}
```

issue_title: Add `id_token_signed_response_alg` when realm default algorithm is not `RS256`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Currently Registration API `GET` request return `id_token_signed_response_alg` only when some algorithm is set for client in `Fine Grain OpenID Connect Configuration` or via `POST`/`PUT` on Registration API endpoint.

If realm administrator set default algorithm for realm it isn't represented in registration API response, so client expects `RS256` as defined in [OpenID Connect Core 1.0 #3.1.3.7](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDTokenValidation) when validating ID token.


### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

When effective ID token signature algorithm is not `RS256` expose effective value via Registration API in `id_token_signed_response_alg` parameter (e.g. to allow client auto-configuration).

### Actual behavior

`id_token_signed_response_alg` is absent if realm default signature algorithm is set and client use realm-default.

### How to Reproduce?

```bash
# in one terminal:
podman run --name -it --rm -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_USER=admin -e KEYCLOAK_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:16.1.0

# in another terminal
podman exec -it kc /bin/bash -l
export PATH=$PATH:/opt/jboss/keycloak/bin

kcadm.sh config credentials --server http://localhost:8080/auth --realm master --user admin --password admin

kcadm.sh create realms -s realm=demo -s enabled=true -s defaultSignatureAlgorithm=ES256

kcadm.sh create users -r demo -s id=da688519-eefe-497a-967a-03c563e097fa -s username=demoadm -s enabled=true
kcadm.sh set-password -r demo --username demoadm --new-password demoadm
kcadm.sh add-roles -r demo --uusername demoadm --cclientid realm-management --rolename realm-admin

kcreg.sh config credentials --server http://localhost:8080/auth --realm demo --user demoadm --password demoadm
kcreg.sh create -s clientId=test-client -s publicClient=false -s standardFlowEnabled=false -s directAccessGrantsEnabled=true
kcreg.sh get test-client -e oidc
```

It returns something like:
```json
{
  "redirect_uris" : [ ],
  "token_endpoint_auth_method" : "client_secret_basic",
  "grant_types" : [ "password", "refresh_token" ],
  "response_types" : [ ],
  "client_id" : "test-client",
  "scope" : "address phone offline_access microprofile-jwt",
  "subject_type" : "public",
  "request_uris" : [ ],
  "tls_client_certificate_bound_access_tokens" : false,
  "client_secret_expires_at" : 0,
  "registration_client_uri" : "http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/demo/clients-registrations/openid-connect/test-client",
  "registration_access_token" : "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICI1MTFjYzQwNi0zODIyLTQ2YzMtYjZiZC05NzVkMTJmOGRjZDUifQ..ISeQ4f3l-SqHyW8AMYI3XLiQI1h8N6LvQExpKKDyVDE",
  "backchannel_logout_session_required" : false,
  "require_pushed_authorization_requests" : false
}
```

Login to http://localhost:8080/auth/admin/demo/console as `demoadm` and check that `id_token` use `ES256`.

### Anything else?

[OpenID Connect Core 1.0 #3.1.3.7](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDTokenValidation) states:

> The `alg` value SHOULD be the default of `RS256` or the algorithm sent by the Client in the `id_token_signed_response_alg` parameter during Registration.
commit_message:
Use realm default signature algorithm for id_token_signed_response_alg

Closes #9695

Signed-off-by: Justin Tay <49700559+justin-tay@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: JPA Map storage doesn't downgrade entityVersion when modifying a row written with a future entityVersion
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the Map JPA store reads data from a row, it accepts data that is written by another Keycloak version with entityVersion N+1. Thereby it ignores new fields written by a later version during JSON de-serialization. 

When it then writes the data back to the database, it uses its JSON serializer and writes the data in the format of its own JSON schema to the row. Therefore, it should downgrade the entityVersion to N as well, so that is future version of Keycloak can again migrate the data to a later entity version. 

### Version

17.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

* when data is written to the row, the entity version is always the SUPPORTED_VERSION_* of the implementation

### Actual behavior

* when data is written to the row, the entity version is kept "as is", thereby leaving the N+1 version in the row. This prevents a future migration of the JSON and is inconsistent with the data in the JSON column.

### How to Reproduce?

Manual testing with the database

### Anything else?

There is a discussion on this topic here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9640

Possible refactoring: Rework repetitive calls to checkEntityVersionForUpdate() in JpaClientEntity to a method annotated with @PreUpdate and @PrePersist, so that is called whenever a SQL insert or update is triggered. 

This would prevent missing an update to the entity version in cases where Hibernate triggers an update to the database.
commit_message:
Update the entityVersion also for downgrades, as it needs to match the JSON and auxiliary tables.

Will trigger also when changes to a child occur, like for example when attributes change.

Closes #9716

issue_title: Updated flag disappearing for nested entities in HotRod store
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Currently, the updated flag for HotRod entities is stored in the delegate. This is working ok for main entities as delegates are enlisted in the transaction directly. However, this doesn't work for nested entities.

The problem is, that the enlisted parent is holding a reference to an entity only, not to its delegate. Hence, when obtaining a nested entity, the delegate is created again with the updated flag set to `false`.

To fix this, we need to store updated flags directly in the entities, not in delegates.

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Store updated flag in the entity, not in the delegate

Closes #9774
